behavior_summary: Provides the AI and interaction handlers for the NPC Salkind. The script implements how Salkind responds when the player 'looks' at him, how he starts/stops scheduled behaviors when the avatar enters/leaves a proximity trigger, how he reacts if the avatar steals from him, and how he responds to being targeted/cast on.

SALKIND::look chooses among short description/bark strings based on global naming/title flags (salkindName, salkindJob) and whether the NPC is dead (Npc::isDead), then issues the chosen string via Item::bark to the querying player.

enterFastArea and leaveFastArea act as trigger-driven schedule control: they reset movement/path references (resetRef) and spawn one of two schedule branches (U8P_SALKIND::04B6 or U8P_SALKIND::05F7) depending on SalkindSchedule and state flags like secondexecution and toranDead.

AvatarStoleSomething marks the NPC as angry (sets salkindPissed), orients/spawns a short pursuit/animation (METHOD::091C) toward the stolen item, barks an accusation, and then invokes the enterFastArea logic to begin active response.

cast handles being targeted by spells or abilities: it checks the NPC's current target, may spawn short face/motion actions and a guard reaction (CALGUARD spawn), and uses randomness and small timers (urandom, FREE::1C19) to vary behavior.

The schedule branches (U8P_SALKIND::04B6 and U8P_SALKIND::05F7) implement walking/animation scripts and periodic barks, using Item::getFrame, Item::getStatus, Npc::pathfind, spawn/METHOD helpers and somebodyTalking gating to coordinate movement and speech.
notable_control_flow: SALKIND::look: branch tree based on global flags salkindName and salkindJob (combined and individual checks) and a Npc::isDead check to select one of several short description strings.

U8P_SALKIND::enterFastArea: early-return if toranDead OR Npc::isDead(self) is true or if secondexecution indicates skipping; otherwise reset multiple refs and spawn a schedule branch chosen by SalkindSchedule (value 1 -> U8P_SALKIND::04B6, value 2 -> U8P_SALKIND::05F7).

AvatarStoleSomething: conditional on schedule and NPC alive state, clears refs, sets salkindPissed, computes direction to stolen item (Item::getDirToItem) and spawns METHOD::091C, barks an accusation, then reuses enterFastArea to start pursuit.

U8P_SALKIND::cast: compares incoming caster/spell id against Npc::getTarget return; may spawn METHOD::15E7 with Npc::getDir and a CALGUARD spawn, and schedules small delays via FREE::1C19; includes randomized bark selection via urandom in some branches.
open_questions: Exact text strings used by SALKIND::look variants and the full set of phrasing differences between salkindName/salkindJob combinations (disassembly indicates variants but literal strings are not all enumerated here).
Meaning and full possible domain of SalkindSchedule beyond observed values (1 and 2) and how schedule variants map to in-game times/behaviors outside this excerpt.
Precise semantics of secondexecution and how it is set elsewhere in the game (it gates enterFastArea behavior but origin is not shown).
Whether toranDead is a global representing a nearby character's death affecting Salkind's schedule, and what game events set/clear it.
Details of what resetRef keys correspond to (which movement/waypoint/state slots are cleared) and how they map to engine-level behavior.

main_function.name: U8P_SALKIND::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the player 'U8P_SALKIND::look' (examine) interaction for NPC Salkind: determine and emit an appropriate short description/bark based on naming/title global flags and the NPC's alive/dead state.
entry_conditions: Invoked when a player or viewer examines/looks at the Salkind NPC (event-driven).
Depends on parameters: pid (viewer/process id) and the NPC process pointer (self).

function_map.name: U8P_SALKIND::look
function_map.role: Respond to player 'U8P_SALKIND::look' events with a short description/bark, choosing wording from flag combinations and dead/alive state.

function_map.name: U8P_SALKIND::schedule
function_map.role: Minimal schedule initializer / placeholder called by the environment to configure process info/exclude; appears to be a trivial process setup.

function_map.name: U8P_SALKIND::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Trigger handler for entering the NPC's fast-area: resets path/state refs and spawns schedule behavior based on SalkindSchedule, respecting toranDead, secondexecution and NPC alive checks.

function_map.name: U8P_SALKIND::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Trigger handler for leaving the fast-area: clears references to stop active schedule/movement.

function_map.name: U8P_SALKIND::AvatarStoleSomething
function_map.role: React to the avatar stealing: if enabled and NPC alive, clear refs, set salkindPissed, orient/spawn a short action toward the stolen item, bark accusation and start pursuit via enterFastArea.

function_map.name: U8P_SALKIND::cast
function_map.role: Handle being targeted/cast on by spells or abilities: check target, spawn short animations, schedule guard/response behaviors, and emit randomized pleas/barks.

function_map.name: U8P_SALKIND::04B6
function_map.role: Primary schedule implementation: parse scripted loop/frame commands, evaluate Item::getFrame/Status, pathfind and spawn movement/animation and speech as part of the schedule.

function_map.name: U8P_SALKIND::05F7
function_map.role: Alternate schedule branch: random timing, orient toward items, spawn short actions and barks; used when SalkindSchedule indicates branch 2.

function_map.name: U8P_SALKIND::072E
function_map.role: Short reaction subroutine used by schedule code to check status bits and possibly bark/animate ('Leave me be!') when interrupted or when someone else is talking.

function_map.name: Npc::isDead
function_map.role: External query used to check whether the NPC is dead to change wording and to block schedule/reactive behavior.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: External routine used to emit short strings/dialog lines to the player/process that triggered the event.

function_map.name: U8P_SALKIND::resetRef
function_map.role: Engine helper to clear internal movement/waypoint/state references for the NPC.

function_map.name: Item::getDirToItem
function_map.role: Compute facing direction from NPC to an item/target; used to orient spawn calls before animations.

function_map.name: METHOD::091C
function_map.role: Spawned helper to perform a facing/short action in a direction (animation/movement).

function_map.name: Npc::getTarget
function_map.role: Return NPC's current interaction/combat target; used by cast to compare against caster/spell id.

function_map.name: Npc::getDir
function_map.role: Return NPC current facing direction; used as input to spawn METHOD reactions.

function_map.name: urandom
function_map.role: Random number generator used to vary timing and dialog selection.

function_map.name: FREE::1C19
function_map.role: Spawned helper used as a delay/timer or light-weight process wrapper to schedule short deferred actions.

function_map.name: Npc::pathfind
function_map.role: Request pathfinding/movement to coordinates or objects during scheduled routines.

internal_calls.purpose: Decide whether to use dead/corpse/late wording and to possibly suppress active reactions; used in branching of the U8P_SALKIND::look text.
Emit the chosen short description string to the player who looked at the NPC.
Clear movement/waypoint references to prepare for starting a schedule branch.
Spawn primary schedule branch when SalkindSchedule indicates branch 1.
Spawn alternate schedule branch when SalkindSchedule indicates branch 2.
Reuse area-entry logic to begin pursuit/response after a theft-triggered bark and spawn.
Invoke short reaction subroutine during schedule path/decision logic when interruptions/status tests require a brief reaction.

external_calls.purpose: Display the selected description string to the specified viewer process (pid).
Determine alive/dead state to choose phrasing and to gate other reactions.
Reset multiple internal state references to stop or reinitialize movement/schedule state.
Compute a facing direction toward the stolen item before spawning a facing/action METHOD.
Spawn a short facing/animation action toward the stolen item (visual/behavioral reaction).
Obtain NPC's current target to compare with incoming caster/spell id and decide reaction.
Get current facing direction to parameterize spawned reaction METHOD::15E7.
Spawn a short movement/animation in a given direction in response to being cast on.
Spawn guard/response behavior (higher-level reaction) when certain cast conditions are met.
Perform movement/pathfinding to scripted coordinates or items as part of schedule playback.
Read frame/animation indices to select schedule actions and coordinates.
Check status bits to gate specific schedule branches or skip actions.

reads.name: salkindName
reads.likely_effect: Select which name variant is used in SALKIND::look (affects wording of the bark).

reads.name: salkindJob
reads.likely_effect: Select whether title/job text (e.g., 'Seneschal') is appended/used in SALKIND::look variants.

reads.name: toranDead
reads.likely_effect: Used with Npc::isDead to decide whether to skip enterFastArea schedule initialization; likely reflects a remote NPC's death that affects behavior.

reads.name: secondexecution
reads.likely_effect: Gates alternate enterFastArea behavior or skips initialization when set; exact origin unclear.

reads.name: SalkindSchedule
reads.likely_effect: Selects which schedule branch to spawn when entering the fast area (observed values map to U8P_SALKIND::04B6 and U8P_SALKIND::05F7).

reads.name: somebodyTalking
reads.likely_effect: Prevents schedule-driven speech/spawned talk actions while someone else is talking.

writes.name: salkindPissed
writes.likely_effect: Set when the avatar steals from Salkind to mark the NPC as angry/alert and trigger pursuit/response behavior.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides the AI and interaction handlers for the NPC Salkind. The script implements how Salkind responds when the player 'looks' at him, how he starts/stops scheduled behaviors when the avatar enters/leaves a proximity trigger, how he reacts if the avatar steals from him, and how he responds to being targeted/cast on.
